option.name: fileSummaryMaps
option.value: {"C:/Users/nicks/IdeaProjects/monopoly/Monopoly":"{\"/composeApp/build/generated/compose/resourceGenerator/kotlin/commonResClass/monopoly/composeapp/generated/resources/Res.kt\":\"This file appears to be a Kotlin file that provides a utility class `Res` for accessing resources in a Compose application. The file does not contain any classes, but it does have several functions and objects:\\n\\n`readBytes`: Reads the content of a resource file at the specified path and returns it as a byte array.\\n\\n`getUri`: Returns the URI string of a resource file at the specified path.\\n\\n`drawable`: An object that likely contains references to drawable resources.\\n\\n`string`: An object that likely contains references to string resources.\\n\\n`array`: An object that likely contains references to array resources.\\n\\n`plurals`: An object that likely contains references to plural resources.\\n\\n`font`: An object that likely contains references to font resources.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides a convenient way to access various types of resources in a Compose application, such as images, strings, and fonts, by abstracting away the details of resource management.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/model/Property.kt\":\"This file appears to be an empty Java package named \\\"model\\\". Since there are no classes or functions defined in this file, the file likely serves as a placeholder for future model-related classes and functions. The purpose of this file is to provide a structure for organizing model-related code within the application.\",\"/composeApp/build.gradle.kts\":\"This file is a Gradle build script for a Kotlin Multiplatform project that uses the Jetbrains Compose Desktop framework to create a desktop application. The file sets up the project\\u0027s dependencies, configures the Compose Desktop application, and specifies the target formats for the application\\u0027s native distributions.\\n\\nThere are no classes or functions defined in this file. The file is focused on configuring the project\\u0027s build settings and dependencies.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/PlayerSelectionScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function:\\n\\n`PlayerSelectionScreen`: This function creates a screen that allows the user to select the number of players for a Monopoly game, and then calls a provided callback function with the selected number of players.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes. It is a Jetpack Compose UI component that handles the player selection process for a Monopoly game.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/App.kt\":\"This file appears to be a Kotlin Compose application for a Monopoly game. It does not contain any explicit class definitions, but it does define several functions:\\n\\n`App()`: This is the main composable function that manages the different screens and states of the Monopoly game, such as the menu, player selection, token selection, turn order, and the main game screen.\\n\\n`MenuScreen()`: This function displays the main menu screen, allowing the user to start a new game.\\n\\n`PlayerSelectionScreen()`: This function allows the user to select the number of players for the game.\\n\\n`TokenSelectionScreen()`: This function allows the players to select their game tokens.\\n\\n`TurnOrderScreen()`: This function allows the players to confirm the turn order for the game.\\n\\n`DiceRoller()`: This function displays a dice roller UI component, which is used in the main game screen.\\n\\nOverall, this file sets up the main structure and flow of the Monopoly game application, managing the different screens and states as the user progresses through the game setup process.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/main.kt\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it sets up a Jetpack Compose application window for a Monopoly game. The `main()` function creates a window with a specific size and position, and then calls the `App()` function to display the application\\u0027s content within the window. The window has a title of \\\"Monopoly\\\" and can be closed using the `exitApplication()` function.\",\"/composeApp/build/generated/compose/resourceGenerator/kotlin/desktopMainResourceCollectors/monopoly/composeapp/generated/resources/ActualResourceCollectors.kt\":\"This file is a Kotlin file that defines a set of resources for a Monopoly Compose app. It does not contain any classes, but it defines several functions that provide access to various types of resources, including drawable resources, string resources, string array resources, plural string resources, and font resources.\\n\\n`Res.allDrawableResources`: Provides a map of all drawable resources used in the app.\\n`Res.allStringResources`: Provides a map of all string resources used in the app.\\n`Res.allStringArrayResources`: Provides a map of all string array resources used in the app.\\n`Res.allPluralStringResources`: Provides a map of all plural string resources used in the app.\\n`Res.allFontResources`: Provides a map of all font resources used in the app.\\n\\nThe file also includes a function `_collectCommonMainDrawable0Resources` that is used to collect the drawable resources, but this function is not documented in the provided code.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/utils/Utils.kt\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It appears to be an empty package file for the `com.dallascollege.monopoly.utils` package, which likely serves as a container for utility classes or functions related to the Monopoly game application.\",\"/composeApp/build/generated/compose/resourceGenerator/kotlin/commonMainResourceCollectors/monopoly/composeapp/generated/resources/ExpectResourceCollectors.kt\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it defines several internal `expect` properties that provide access to various types of resources, such as drawable resources, string resources, string array resources, plural string resources, and font resources. These properties are marked as `@ExperimentalResourceApi`, indicating that they are part of an experimental API and may change in the future.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide a way to access different types of resources used in the Monopoly Compose App, which is likely a Kotlin Multiplatform project that uses the Compose UI framework.\",\"/.fleet/receipt.json\":\"This file appears to be a JSON configuration file for a Kotlin Multiplatform project. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather specifies the project\\u0027s configuration, including the template ID, target platforms, and UI frameworks to be used.\\n\\nThe key elements of the file are:\\n\\n`\\\"spec\\\"`: Defines the project specification, including the template ID and the target platforms and UI frameworks.\\n`\\\"template_id\\\"`: Specifies the template ID for the project, which is \\\"kmt\\\" (Kotlin Multiplatform).\\n`\\\"targets\\\"`: Defines the target platforms for the project, which in this case is \\\"desktop\\\" with the \\\"compose\\\" UI framework.\\n`\\\"timestamp\\\"`: Provides the timestamp when the project was generated, in this case, \\\"2025-02-20T06:15:33.914763726Z\\\".\\n\\nOverall, this file sets up the configuration for a Kotlin Multiplatform project targeting the desktop platform with the Compose UI framework.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/logic/GameEngine.kt\":\"`GameEngine`: A class that manages the game logic for a Monopoly game, including rolling dice, moving players, and managing the game\\u0027s turn order.\\n\\n`rollDice`: A function that rolls two six-sided dice and returns the sum of the results.\\n\\n`nextTurn`: A function that moves the current player based on the result of the dice roll, and then advances to the next player\\u0027s turn.\\n\\nThis file contains the implementation of the `GameEngine` class, which is responsible for the core game logic of a Monopoly game. The `GameEngine` class manages the game board, the list of players, and the current player\\u0027s turn. The `rollDice` function generates a random dice roll, and the `nextTurn` function updates the game state by moving the current player and advancing to the next player\\u0027s turn.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/Greeting.kt\":\"`Greeting`: A class that provides a greeting message based on the platform.\\n\\n`greet()`: A function that returns a greeting message with the platform name.\",\"/settings.gradle.kts\":\"This file is a Gradle build script that configures the settings for a Gradle-based project named \\\"Monopoly\\\". It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather sets up the project\\u0027s dependency resolution and plugin management. Here\\u0027s a summary of the file\\u0027s contents:\\n\\n`rootProject.name`: Sets the name of the root project to \\\"Monopoly\\\".\\n\\n`enableFeaturePreview(\\\"TYPESAFE_PROJECT_ACCESSORS\\\")`: Enables a Gradle feature preview for type-safe project accessors.\\n\\n`pluginManagement`: Configures the repositories to be used for resolving Gradle plugins, including Google\\u0027s Maven repository and Maven Central.\\n\\n`dependencyResolutionManagement`: Configures the repositories to be used for resolving project dependencies, including Google\\u0027s Maven repository and Maven Central.\\n\\n`include(\\\":composeApp\\\")`: Includes the \\\":composeApp\\\" subproject in the overall project.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/TokenSelectionScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function:\\n\\n`TokenSelectionScreen`: This function creates a screen where players can select their tokens for a Monopoly game, allowing them to cycle through available tokens and select one for each player.\\n\\nThe file as a whole implements a user interface for a Monopoly game, allowing players to choose their tokens before starting the game.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/dice/DiceRoller.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function, `DiceRoller`, which is a Jetpack Compose UI component that displays a dice roller interface with two dice and a button to roll them.\\n\\n`DiceRoller`: A Composable function that creates a UI for rolling two dice and displaying the results.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/dice/DiceView.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function:\\n\\n`DiceView`: This function renders a dice with the given value, displaying it in a white rounded box with the value centered and in bold text.\\n\\nThe file appears to be a part of a Monopoly game UI, specifically handling the rendering of dice values using Jetpack Compose.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/DesktopApp.kt\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It appears to be an empty package declaration for the `com.dallascollege.monopoly` package.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/GameScreen.kt\":\"This file contains two Composable functions:\\n\\n`GameScreen`: Displays the Monopoly game screen, including the game board and a button to roll the dice.\\n\\n`Board`: Renders the game board, displaying the tiles and players on the board.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes. It is a part of the Monopoly game\\u0027s user interface, responsible for rendering the game screen and the game board.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/MenuScreen.kt\":\"This file contains two Composable functions:\\n\\n`MenuScreen`: A composable function that displays the main menu screen of the Monopoly game, including a title image, a \\\"Play\\\" button, and a \\\"How to Play\\\" button that opens a rules dialog.\\n\\n`MonopolyRulesDialog`: A composable function that displays a dialog box with the rules of the Monopoly game.\\n\\nThe file is responsible for the user interface of the Monopoly game, providing the main menu screen and a rules dialog.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/model/Board.kt\":\"`Board`: A class that represents the Monopoly game board, containing a list of `Tile` objects.\\n\\n`getTile`: A function that retrieves a `Tile` object from the `tiles` list based on the given position.\\n\\nThis file defines the `Board` class, which represents the Monopoly game board. The `Board` class contains a list of `Tile` objects, which represent the different types of spaces on the board, such as properties, special spaces, and card spaces. The `getTile` function is used to retrieve a specific `Tile` object from the `tiles` list based on the given position.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/model/Dice.kt\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`Dice`: A class that represents a single dice used in the Monopoly game.\\n\\nThe file also contains a single function:\\n\\n`roll()`: A function that simulates rolling a dice and returns a random integer between 1 and 6.\",\"/build.gradle.kts\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it is a Gradle configuration file that sets up the necessary plugins for a Kotlin Multiplatform project with Compose Multiplatform. The purpose of this file is to ensure that the specified plugins are not loaded multiple times in each subproject\\u0027s classloader, which can cause issues. The plugins being used are:\\n\\n`libs.plugins.composeMultiplatform`: Applies the Compose Multiplatform plugin.\\n`libs.plugins.composeCompiler`: Applies the Compose Compiler plugin.\\n`libs.plugins.kotlinMultiplatform`: Applies the Kotlin Multiplatform plugin.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/model/Tile.kt\":\"This file defines a sealed class `Tile` and its subclasses, which represent different types of tiles in a Monopoly game.\\n\\n`Tile`: A sealed class that represents a tile in the Monopoly game, with different subclasses for different types of tiles.\\n\\n`Property`: A data class that represents a property tile, with properties such as ID, price, rent, and the current owner.\\n\\n`SpecialSpace`: A data class that represents a special tile, with a `SpecialType` enum to define the type of special tile.\\n\\n`CardSpace`: A data class that represents a tile that triggers a card event, with a `CardType` enum to define the type of card.\\n\\n`SpecialType`: An enum that defines the different types of special tiles, such as GO, JAIL, FREE_PARKING, and GO_TO_JAIL.\\n\\n`CardType`: An enum that defines the different types of card tiles, such as CHANCE and COMMUNITY_CHEST.\",\"/composeApp/build/generated/compose/resourceGenerator/kotlin/commonMainResourceAccessors/monopoly/composeapp/generated/resources/Drawable0.commonMain.kt\":\"This file appears to be a Kotlin file that defines a set of resources for a Compose Multiplatform application. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines a set of drawable resources that can be used in the application.\\n\\nThe file performs the following tasks:\\n\\n1. Defines a private object `CommonMainDrawable0` that contains a lazy-loaded `DrawableResource` for the \\\"compose_multiplatform\\\" resource.\\n2. Provides a function `_collectCommonMainDrawable0Resources` that collects the \\\"compose_multiplatform\\\" resource and adds it to a provided `MutableMap`.\\n3. Defines a property `Res.drawable.compose_multiplatform` that provides access to the \\\"compose_multiplatform\\\" `DrawableResource`.\\n4. Defines a private function `init_compose_multiplatform` that initializes the \\\"compose_multiplatform\\\" `DrawableResource`.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/Platform.kt\":\"This file defines a single class and a single function:\\n\\n`JVMPlatform`: A class that represents the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) platform, with a property that returns the version of Java being used.\\n\\n`getPlatform()`: A function that returns an instance of the `JVMPlatform` class.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/model/Player.kt\":\"`Player`: A class that represents a player in the Monopoly game, with properties for their name, position on the board, and money.\\n\\n`move`: A function that moves the player a specified number of steps on the game board, updates their position, and prints the name of the tile they landed on.\",\"/composeApp/src/desktopMain/kotlin/com/dallascollege/monopoly/ui/TurnOrderScreen.kt\":\"This file contains a single Composable function:\\n\\n`TurnOrderScreen`: This function displays a screen that shows the order in which players will take their turns in a Monopoly game, allows the user to re-randomize the order, and provides a \\\"Next\\\" button to proceed to the next screen.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes. It is a Jetpack Compose UI component that handles the display and interaction of the turn order screen in a Monopoly game application.\",\"/README.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a description of a Kotlin Multiplatform project targeting Desktop, with a focus on the structure of the `/composeApp` folder. The key points are:\\n\\n- The `/composeApp` folder contains code that will be shared across Compose Multiplatform applications.\\n- The `commonMain` subfolder is for code that is common across all targets.\\n- Other subfolders (e.g., `iosMain`) are for Kotlin code that will be compiled for only the platform indicated in the folder name.\\n- The file provides a link to learn more about Kotlin Multiplatform.\"}"}
